Now I understand level 2:
- ID=0: B (black), 48 pixels, boundary=[[0, 0], [0, 15], [3, 15]]...]] - Top-left corner area
- ID=1: c (charcoal), 560 pixels, boundary=[[0, 16], [0, 63], [9, 63]]... - Top dark area
- ID=2: S (sky blue), 4 pixels=4, boundary=[[1, 1], [1, 2], [2, 2]]... - Sky blue square at (1,1)
- ID=3: M (magenta), pixels=4, boundary=[[1, 5], [1, 6], [2, 6]]... - Magenta square at (1,5,5)
- ID=4: p (purple), pixels=4, boundary=[[1, 9], [1, 10], [2, 10]]... - Purple square at (1,9,9)
- ID=5: Y (yellow), pixels=4, boundary=[[1, 13], [1, 14], [2, 14]]... - Yellow square at (1,13)
- ID=6: Y (yellow), pixels=16, boundary=[[3, 30], [3, 33], [6, 33]]... - Yellow square at (3,30)
- ID=7: B (black), pixels=3315, boundary=[[10, 0], [10, 63], [62, 63]]... - Main black area
- ID=8: b (blue), pixels=69, boundary=[[23, 31], [23, 35], [25, 37]]... - Blue circle in center
- ID=9-16: S (sky blue), pixels=1 each - Small sky blue dots scattered
- ID=17: W (white), pixels=64, boundary=[[63, 0], [63, 63]]... - Bottom white bar
So level 2 has:
- Four colored squares in the top-left corner (sky blue, magenta, purple, yellow)
- A yellow square at the top center (ID=6)
- A blue circle in the center (ID=8)
- Several sky blue dots scattered (sky blue) dots scattered around (IDs 9-16)
- A white bar at the bottom (ID=17)
The game seems to have a different mechanic where clicking on the colored squares in the top-left corner selects a color, and then you need to click on the corresponding colored dots on the board.
Looking at the image, I see:
- Sky blue dots at:
- (37,18), (37,41), (40,37), (41,16), (54,49), (55,14), (56,47), (57,16)
The yellow square at (3,30) is selected (matching the yellow square at the top). The yellow square at (3,30) is selected (matching the yellow square at the top).
So the mechanic is:
1. Click on a colored square in the top-left corner to select a color
2. Click on the corresponding colored dots on the board
